name: the primitive type called are

phase_0.boundary_condition: The holographic screen containing the complete bulk projection

VINIT.justification: The state of the bulk before any boundary correspondence is established.

TANCH.justification: The terminal boundary that contains the entirety of the bulk information.

AFWD.justification: The forward morphism from the bulk interior to the boundary surface.

AREV.justification: The reverse morphism reconstructing the bulk from boundary data.

CLINK.justification: The composition of local boundary operations to simulate bulk movement.

IMSCRIB.justification: The recognition that the boundary is the bulk in a different representation.

FSPLIT.justification: Splitting the system into a boundary-only representation and a bulk-only representation.

FFUSE.justification: Reconstituting the unified holographic state from the boundary and bulk representations.

EVALT.justification: The state where boundary data perfectly recovers the bulk state.

EVALF.justification: The state where the boundary projection is incomplete or decoherent.

ENGAGR.justification: The simultaneous existence of the system as both interior and surface.

IFIX.justification: The permanent fixation of the winding number or holographic state.

phase_3.void_description: uninitialized manifold without defined dimensionality or topology
phase_3.true_description: successful holographic encoding with full bulk-boundary correspondence
phase_3.false_description: failed projection where boundary data is insufficient to map the bulk
phase_3.both_description: entangled state where bulk and boundary are indistinguishable

synthesize the boundary and bulk into a unified holographic state
hold the bulk and boundary in simultaneous entangled correspondence
